prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='CWT_rf', seasonality_champion='FT_catboost', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.4862) and very high seasonality variance (0.7777), indicating models capture patterns very differently. FT_rf dominates as overall champion with lowest RMSE, while CWT_rf leads in trend tracking and FT_catboost excels at seasonality. Early and late horizon specialists differ significantly, supporting per-horizon selection. Given both high disagreement AND high seasonality variance, DBA combination is the optimal approach to leverage diverse model behaviors.

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "CWT_rf",
  "seasonality_champion": "FT_catboost",
  "overall_champion": "FT_rf",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "late": "FT_rf"},
  "tier1_models": ["FT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "CWT_rf", "DWT_rf", "CWT_catboost", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "NaiveMovingAverage", "CWT_svr"],
  "tier2_models": ["ONLY_FT_svr", "THETA", "ONLY_FT_rf", "ETS", "FT_svr", "ARIMA", "FT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.4862,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["down", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.4862) and very high seasonality variance (0.7777), indicating models capture patterns very differently. FT_rf dominates as overall champion with lowest RMSE, while CWT_rf leads in trend tracking and FT_catboost excels at seasonality. Early and late horizon specialists differ significantly, supporting per-horizon selection. Given both high disagreement AND high seasonality variance, DBA combination is the optimal approach to leverage diverse model behaviors."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25", "exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim0.8"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25": {"top_k": 3, "shrinkage": 0.25}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=8 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection. relative_spread_mean=0.382 (≥0.25) and rmse_spread_ratio=0.4862 signal high model disagreement favoring DBA combination as recommended_method_hint. RMSE gap between FT_rf (2098.54) and ONLY_DWT_svr (3181.86) is 51.6% (>30%), justifying inverse_rmse_weights with shrinkage. n_windows=3 requires small top_k=3 and shrinkage≥0.3 for stability. FT_catboost seasonality_champion and CWT_rf trend_champion diversity captured by DBA."
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.4862) and very high seasonality variance (0.7777), indicating models capture patterns very differently. FT_rf dominates as overall champion with lowest RMSE, while CWT_rf leads in trend tracking and FT_catboost excels at seasonality. Early and late horizon specialists differ significantly, supporting per-horizon selection. Given both high disagreement AND high seasonality variance, DBA combination is the optimal approach to leverage diverse model behaviors.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=8 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection. relative_spread_mean=0.382 (≥0.25) and rmse_spread_ratio=0.4862 signal high model disagreement favoring DBA combination as recommended_method_hint. RMSE gap between FT_rf (2098.54) and ONLY_DWT_svr (3181.86) is 51.6% (>30%), justifying inverse_rmse_weights with shrinkage. n_windows=3 requires small top_k=3 and shrinkage≥0.3 for stability. FT_catboost seasonality_champion and CWT_rf trend_champion diversity captured by DBA.
